content: Just as the common language-learning definition of reflexion frames it as a metacognitive step to surface unspoken gaps in understanding and refine language output, the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework can be extended to ground LLM self-reflection mechanisms in graded, language-learning-style reflective exercises rather than just contrastive solving perspectives. This creates a scaffolded self-reflection pipeline that mimics how humans gradually refine their reasoning through iterative guided reflection, rather than jumping directly to contrasting inconsistent outputs.
